name: Agent-First Slug
synopsis: Rust slug generation with explicit caller configuration for path and URL path segments.
intent: Provide a reusable slug primitive where callers configure character classes, dots, transliteration, truncation, empty behavior, and validation instead of relying on hard-coded product rules
Keep the library generic: no presets, downstream project names, hidden fallback words, or built-in legacy modes
Provide a small afslug CLI that applies the default rules and emits AFDATA protocol events for one-off use
Publish the agent-first-slug skill with explicit Rust library and afslug CLI workflows
Make slug generation deterministic and test-covered so callers can preserve or migrate their own identifiers intentionally
mutations: Restructure the afslug CLI into slugify and validate subcommands (breaking: bare 'afslug <INPUT>' is now 'afslug slugify <INPUT>').
Expose the full SlugConfig surface as slugify flags and add validate for local/URL path segments; transliteration stays library-only.
